query_normalized: investigating the impact of code ownership on llm code understanding computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on "context engineering" and multi-agent workflows to improve LLM performance on complex codebases, but it does not measure, analyze, or discuss "code ownership" (e.g., developer attribution, ownership metrics, or the impact of ownership structures) as a variable affecting LLM understanding. It addresses a different mechanism (context retrieval and agent orchestration) rather than the specific construct of code ownership requested by the user.
